chunks.page_summary: This page provides a step-by-step installation guide for the Rheonics Control Panel (RCP) application, detailing how to prepare the installation source and proceed through the setup wizard.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863
Installing the Rheonics Control Panel (RCP) Application
Modified on: Fri, 3 Jul, 2020 at 11:51 AM

The installation software is supplied on a USB drive (USB stick, flash drive). Updates can also be downloaded from Rheonics website, contact support.

Open the folder on the USB drive to view files. If the system asks whether to repair the disk, select the “Open without repairing” option. Before proceeding, copy the files to a folder on your computer, and remove the USB stick and put it in a secure place.

The newly created installation folder will contain the following Setup files. Execute the RCPSetup_Vx.x.x.x_full.exe file.

Name                     | Date modified
-------------------------|------------------
Manuals                  | 13/02/2020 12:36
Win7_USB_Driver          | 29/08/2018 12:31
RCPSetup_V4.3.0.2_full   | 5/03/2020 11:47
Readme                   | 16/04/2020 06:32
Transmitter Display Module and USB Inst... | 23/07/2018 01:06

The following procedure explains the full installation of the RCP software:
1. Double click on “RCPSetup_Vx.x.x.x_full.exe” to start the installation process.
2. The installer will start the Setup Wizard Application. Click “Next” to continue the installation process.

3. A dialog will appear to select the installation directories. Use the default, unless there is a reason to select a different location, and click “Next”:
key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel (RCP) installation software is provided on a USB drive.
Updates for the software are available for download from the Rheonics website.
Users must copy installation files from the USB drive to their computer before proceeding with installation.
The main installation executable is named `RCPSetup_Vx.x.x.x_full.exe` (e.g., `RCPSetup_V4.3.0.2_full`).
The default installation path for Rheonics Control Panel is `C:\Program Files\RheonicsControlPanel`.
The installation requires at least 273.0 MB of free disk space.
diagram_labels: Where should Rheonics Control Panel be installed?
Setup will install Rheonics Control Panel into the following folder.
To continue, click Next. If you would like to select a different folder, click Browse.
At least 273.0 MB of free disk space is required.
warnings_notes: If the system prompts to repair the disk when opening the USB drive, select the “Open without repairing” option.
Before installation, copy the files from the USB stick to a folder on your computer and then remove the USB stick, storing it securely.
When selecting installation directories, it is recommended to use the default unless there is a specific reason to choose a different location.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides step-by-step instructions for installing the Rheonics Control Panel software, including options for desktop shortcuts, file extraction, and handling the installation of the LabVIEW Runtime Engine using the NI Package Manager.
chunks.extracted_text:
4. Click check box to create a Desktop shortcut, and click “Next”:
Setup - Rheonics Control Panel
Select Additional Tasks
Which additional tasks should be performed?
Select the additional tasks you would like Setup to perform while installing Rheonics Control Panel, then click Next.
Additional shortcuts:
Create a desktop shortcut
< Back
Next >
Cancel

5. Click “Install” to complete the installation process of RCP
Setup - Rheonics Control Panel
Ready to Install
Setup is now ready to begin installing Rheonics Control Panel on your computer.
Click Install to continue with the installation, or click Back if you want to review or change any settings.
Destination location:
C:\Program Files\Rheonics\ControlPanel
Additional tasks:
Additional shortcuts:
Create a desktop shortcut
< Back
Install
Cancel

6. The installer will extract the files in the default destination.
Setup - Rheonics Control Panel
Installing
Please wait while Setup installs Rheonics Control Panel on your computer.
Extracting files...
C:\Program Files\LabVIEWRunTimeEngine2012.exe
Cancel

7. When the files are copied. The wizard will display the message "installing LabVIEW runtime engine". This will prompt a new window with the "NI Package Manager" software.
NI Package Manager
Loading...
© 2006-2019 National Instruments. All rights reserved.
NATIONAL INSTRUMENTS

8. If you get the window below, it means that LabVIEW Runtime Engine is already installed. Click the X at the top right of the window to close it and continue from step 12. Otherwise follow the instructions from step 9.
key_facts: Users must select the option to create a desktop shortcut for the Rheonics Control Panel during the installation process.
The installation process for the Rheonics Control Panel (RCP) is completed by clicking the 'Install' button.
The installer automatically extracts necessary files, including 'LabVIEWRunTimeEngine2012.exe', to the default destination.
The 'NI Package Manager' software handles the installation of the LabVIEW Runtime Engine.
If the 'NI Package Manager' window appears with 'Loading...', it indicates that the LabVIEW Runtime Engine is already installed.
If the LabVIEW Runtime Engine is already installed, users should close the 'NI Package Manager' window and proceed to step 12.
diagram_labels: Which additional tasks should be performed?
Select the additional tasks you would like Setup to perform while installing Rheonics Control Panel, then click Next.
Setup is now ready to begin installing Rheonics Control Panel on your computer.
Click Install to continue with the installation, or click Back if you want to review or change any settings.
Please wait while Setup installs Rheonics Control Panel on your computer.
warnings_notes: If LabVIEW Runtime Engine is not already installed, users should follow instructions from step 9.
If LabVIEW Runtime Engine is already installed, click the X at the top right of the window to close it and continue from step 12.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides step-by-step instructions for installing or verifying the installation of National Instruments VISA runtime components and the Rheonics Control Panel software.
chunks.extracted_text:
12. The installer will display the message "Installing VISA runtime engine", with a progress bar
    Setup - Rheonics Control Panel
    Installing
    Please wait while Setup installs Rheonics Control Panel on your computer.
    Installing VISA Runtime 2019
    Cancel

13. A window will display a list of items to install. Click on the "Deselect All" button and click "Next".
    Installing
    Select
    Agree
    Review
    Finish
    Additional items you may wish to install:
    NI Certificates Installer
    NI Certificates configures Microsoft Windows to always trust software from NI. When NI Certificates
    are installed, no Windows security popups appear for installers that have a valid National Instruments
    digital signature.
    NI I/O Trace
    Debugging utility for monitoring function calls to various National Instruments APIs.
    NI PXI Platform Services Runtime
    NI PXI Platform Services Runtime supports NI PXI Chassis and Controllers, implements the PXI
    specifications, and supplies system services to other NI products.
    NI-VISA .NET Development Support
    Installs the local assembly for development of NI-VISA applications using the standard .NET API
    specified by the IVI Foundation.
    NI-VISA .NET Runtime
    Enables you to run applications that use the NI-VISA .NET API.
    NI-VISA C Examples
    Provides examples for programming using the C API
    Select All
    Deselect All
    Next

14. If the window below is displayed, then VISA runtime is already installed in your system. Click on the X at the top right corner of the window, and ignore the steps below.
    Installing
    Select
    Agree
    Review
    Finish
    No operation to be performed.

15. Afterwards, the list of items that will be installed is presented. Make sure that the "NI-Visa Runtime" is present, and click "Next"
key_facts: The installer for Rheonics Control Panel will display a progress bar for "Installing VISA runtime engine" and specifically mentions "Installing VISA Runtime 2019".
During the installation of additional items, users are instructed to click "Deselect All" and then "Next".
If the installation window shows "No operation to be performed", it indicates that the VISA runtime is already installed on the system.
If VISA runtime is already installed, users should close the window by clicking the 'X' and ignore subsequent installation steps related to it.
Before proceeding with the final installation, it is crucial to ensure that "NI-Visa Runtime" is listed among the items to be installed.
After confirming the presence of "NI-Visa Runtime", the user should click "Next" to continue.
diagram_labels: Please wait while Setup installs Rheonics Control Panel on your computer.
Additional items you may wish to install:
warnings_notes: If the 'No operation to be performed' window is displayed, click on the X at the top right corner of the window, and ignore the steps below.

Installed an old version and looking to update to new version:
key_facts: The installation process involves reviewing a summary of components and accepting a software license agreement.
The software components being installed include NI Update Service, NI-VISA, NI-VISA Runtime, and Microsoft .NET Framework 4.6.2.
Users must accept the National Instruments Software License Agreement to proceed with the installation.
A system reboot is required to finalize the installation process.
Upon successful installation, RCP Software, LabVIEW runtime, and NI-VISA runtime will be ready for use.
Users are instructed to decline periodic update checks from the NI Update Service during installation.
diagram_labels: Review the following summary before continuing.
You must accept the license agreements below to proceed.
I accept the above 2 license agreements.
I do not accept all the license agreements.
NI Update Service checks for available updates each time you install new NI software. Would you like NI Update Service also to check for updates periodically?
Note: If updates are available, you can choose whether to install or ignore them. Updates are never installed automatically.
warnings_notes: YOU SHOULD REBOOT TO FINISH THE INSTALL.
If you get prompted with the NI Update Service window, click "No" to decline periodic update checks.
Note: If updates are available, you can choose whether to install or ignore them. Updates are never installed automatically.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides instructions for users with an old version of Rheonics RCP software on how to update to a new version using the RCP_Lite_VX and directs them to a support article for the RCP Lite Installer.
chunks.extracted_text:
If you have installed an old version of RCP, and want to do an update for a new version, You could use the "RCP_Lite_VX."
Check the solution article - Installing RCP Lite Installer (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000296)
key_facts: Users with an old version of RCP software who wish to update to a new version can use the "RCP_Lite_VX" tool.
A solution article titled "Installing RCP Lite Installer" is available for further guidance.
The solution article can be found at the URL: https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000296.
warnings_notes: It is advised to check the solution article for comprehensive instructions on installing the RCP Lite Installer.
